title: bulbophyllum keralense
intro: Bulbophyllum keralense, also known as the Kerala Bulbophyllum, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to the Western Ghats mountain range in India. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and combating infections. In Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is prepared in herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions to treat various ailments, and is also used in traditional remedies for skin and respiratory issues.
